
```fortran 
module gg
    contains
    function func_arr3(inp_ref) result(ret_arr)
        integer, intent(in) :: inp_ref
        integer,allocatable :: ret_arr(:)
        ! print *, allocated(ret_arr)
        allocate(ret_arr(1))
        ret_arr = [inp_ref]
        print *, "worked", inp_ref
    end function
end module


program p
    use gg
    implicit none
    integer, allocatable :: inp(:)
    integer :: i =77
    
    do i= 1, 3
        inp = func_arr3(i)
    end do 
end program  
end program  
```
```Console
assem@assem-PC:~/Desktop/fortran/compile_gsnap$ gfortran tt6.f90 && ./a.out 
 worked           1
 worked           2
 worked           3
assem@assem-PC:~/Desktop/fortran/compile_gsnap$ lfortran tt6.f90 --experimental-simplifier
worked    77
assem@assem-PC:~/Desktop/fortran/compile_gsnap$ lfortran tt6.f90 
worked    1
worked    2
worked    3

The results from gfortran were OK, but from lfortran 0.42.0 were not. The program:
```
module gg ! file alloc.f90
    contains
    function func_arr3(inp_ref) result(ret_arr)
        integer, intent(in) :: inp_ref
        integer,allocatable :: ret_arr(:)
        print *, 'allocated on entry?', allocated(ret_arr)
!!        allocate(ret_arr(1))
        ret_arr = [inp_ref,inp_ref+1]
        print *, "worked", ret_arr !! inp_ref
        print *,'allocated on exit ?',allocated(ret_arr)
    end function
end module

program p
    use gg, only: func_arr3
    implicit none
    integer, allocatable :: inp(:)
    integer :: i !! =77
    
    do i= 1, 3
       inp = func_arr3(i)
       print *,'inp = ',inp
    end do 
end program  
```
Results:
```
(lf) john:~$ gfortran alloc.f90 && ./a.out
 allocated on entry? F
 worked           1           2
 allocated on exit ? T
 inp =            1           2
 allocated on entry? F
 worked           2           3
 allocated on exit ? T
 inp =            2           3
 allocated on entry? F
 worked           3           4
 allocated on exit ? T
 inp =            3           4
(lf) john:~$ lfortran alloc.f90 
allocated on entry?    F
worked

allocated on exit ?    F
inp = 

allocated on entry?    F
worked

allocated on exit ?    F
inp = 

allocated on entry?    F
worked

allocated on exit ?    F
inp = 

(lf) john:~$ 
````

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5707","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5707/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5707/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5707/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5707","id":2740383295,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6jVu4_","number":5707,"title":"allocate statement disallowed!","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-12-15T07:51:08Z","updated_at":"2024-12-15T07:51:08Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This program uses integer arrays ```x(0:2)``` and allocatable ```y(:)```, which is allocated with the same bounds.
```
  integer x(0:2)
  integer,allocatable::y(:)
  x(:) = [1,2,3]
  x(1:) = [10,20]
  print "(3(1X,I0))",x
  allocate(y(0:2))
  y(:) = [1,2,3]
  y(1:) = [10,20]
  print "(3(1X,I0))",y
end program
```
Lfortran 0.42.0 misinterorets the allocate statement.
```
 + lfortran --std=f23 -o badarray.f90oL badarray.f90
syntax error: Statement or Declaration expected inside program, found Expression
 --> badarray.f90:6:3
  |
6 |   allocate(y(0:2))
  |   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 
```
Run-time outputs from gfortran, g95, ifort, ifx and AMD flang are identical and correct:
```
 1 10 20
 1 10 20

```

```

```
Four other compilers gave this output, which IMHO is correct:
```

 5 6
```

been misled by assuming that the ```--std=f23``` option would enforce compliance with the Fortran standard I suggest that using that option should automatically use all the other options that make lfortran accept programs that comply with the standard, whether or not they agree with the opinions of lfortran's developers on what is good practice. I think the relevant options are
```
--realloc-lhs 
--use-loop-variable-after-loop 
--implicit-typing
--implicit-interface
```
but I may have missed some, or included some that would allow a non-conforming program.
If that is unacceptable to the developers I suggest that those options and ```--std=f23``` be included in a new option, possibly called ```--strict``` ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2547713960/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

The issue arose from a comment by @certik in #5739. The loop variable ```i``` has the wrong value after one of the loops; which loop it is depends on an option.
```
  integer i
  do i = 1,2
     print "(A,I0)",'In loop 1 i=',i
  end do
  print "(A,I0,A)",'After loop 1 i=',i,merge(' good',' bad ',i==3)
  do i = 1,2
     print "(A,I0)",'In loop 2 i=',i
     if (i<2) exit
  end do
  print "(A,I0,A)",'After loop 2 i=',i,merge(' good',' bad ',i==1)
  end program
```
Output with no options or ```--std=f23```
```
In loop 1 i=1
In loop 1 i=2
After loop 1 i=2 bad 
In loop 2 i=1
After loop 2 i=1 good
```
Output with ```--use-loop-variable-after-loop``` with or without ```--std=f23```
```
In loop 1 i=1
In loop 1 i=2
After loop 1 i=3 good
In loop 2 i=1
After loop 2 i=2 bad 
```
Output (correct IMHO) from 5 other compilers: gfortran, g95, ifx, ifort, AMD flang
```
In loop 1 i=1
In loop 1 i=2
After loop 1 i=3 good
In loop 2 i=1
After loop 2 i=1 good
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5761/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5761/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

- [ ] PRIMA
- [x] POT3D
- [x] Legacy Minpack (SciPy)
- [x] Modern Minpack (Fortran-Lang)
- [x] Modern Minpack (check results)
- [x] dftatom
- [x] dftatom (with `--fast`)
- [x] fastGPT
- [x] fastGPT (with `--fast`)
- [x] fpm
- [x] stdlib
- [x] SNAP
- [x] SNAP (with `--fast`)

Basic intent is to make everything work with `--experimental-simplifier` which used to work with older LFortran.

`predsci/POT3D`"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-12-15T11:15:57Z","updated_at":"2024-12-22T07:30:08Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Minimal Reproducible Example

For the below Fortran program:
```f90
program POT3D
    use iso_fortran_env, only : OUTPUT_UNIT
    implicit none
    print *, OUTPUT_UNIT
    call FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT) ! I used 'call FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT)' instead of just 'FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT)'
end

```

on it's execution with LFortran, I get the below error:
```console
> lfortran --version
LFortran version: 0.42.0-526-g75e4b770b
Platform: macOS ARM
LLVM: 11.1.0
Default target: arm64-apple-darwin23.6.0

> lfortran test.f90
semantic error: Function 'flush' not found (not user defined nor intrinsic)
  --> test.f90:92:5
   |
92 |     call FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT)
   |     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```

if I change `call FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT)` to `FLUSH(OUTPUT_UNIT)` I get a different error:
```console
6
Specified UNIT 6 in FLUSH is not connected.

> echo $?
1
```

in `POT3D` code, I only see it's call as `call FLUSH(...)`, that's why reporting it here in that way.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5709/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5709/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2558360798","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5709#issuecomment-2558360798","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5709","id":2558360798,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6YfXze","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-22T07:30:07Z","updated_at":"2024-12-22T07:30:07Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"The FLUSH statement first appeared in the F2003 standard. No FLUSH intrinsic is in any Fortran standard. It does not work with g95 (so it must have appeared in gfortran after the fork) , ifx, ifort or AMD flang. I think lfortran developers have better things to do with their time than implementing a gfortran feature that even gfortran's own website recommends should not be used. Also, note that
the FLUSH statement allows ```iostat=``` and ```iomsg=``` but the intrinsic does not.

```fortran
module a
use iso_fortran_env, only: dp => real64
end module

program test_dp
use a
integer(dp) :: i
end program
```
This gives:
```console
$ gfortran a.f90 
$ lfortran a.f90 
semantic error: Variable 'dp' is not declared
 --> a.f90:7:9
  |
7 | integer(dp) :: i
  |         ^^ 'dp' is undeclared
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5870/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5870/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2564832629","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5870#issuecomment-2564832629","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5870","id":2564832629,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Y4D11","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-29T20:34:43Z","updated_at":"2024-12-29T20:34:43Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Providing ```, only: dp``` made the program compile. ```
module a
use iso_fortran_env, only: dp => real64
end module a

real(dp) :: x = 42

end program test_dp
```

It prints correct results only with the first three of those compilers.
```
program readexpinf ! file readexpinf.f90
  implicit none
  real     :: inf
  character:: cinf*3 = 'INF'
  read(cinf,*) inf
  print "(A,1X,E10.2)","reading 'INF' = ",inf
  print "(A,1X,I10)"  ,'exponent(inf) = ',exponent(inf)
end program readexpinf
```
The run-time result from gfortran, ifort or ifx:
```
Reading 'INF' gives   Infinity
exponent(inf)     = 2147483647
```
The run-time result from lfortran 0.41.0:
```
Reading 'INF' gives   .000E+00
exponent(inf)     =          0
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5274/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5274/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341","id":2649238783,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6d6Cz_","number":5341,"title":"Possibly incorrect way of data initialization","user":{"login":"gxyd","id":7712241,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjc3MTIyNDE=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/7712241?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd","html_url":"https://github.com/gxyd","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gxyd/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-11-11T12:45:28Z","updated_at":"2024-11-11T20:43:06Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Minimal Reproducible Example

The below fortran program is derived from [tests/fixedform_doloop3.f](https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/blob/main/tests/fixedform_doloop3.f), which I think has an incorrect initialization:
```f90
SUBROUTINE START1()
    IMPLICIT NONE
    INTEGER :: L
    INTEGER :: N
    REAL F, ONE=1, TWO=2
END

program main
end program main

```

the original line in the program `tests/fixedform_doloop3.f` is:
```f90
        REAL F(L), ONE=1, TWO=2
```
here: https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/blob/main/tests/fixedform_doloop3.f#L7

## GFortran output
```console
> gfortran test.f90 -o a.out && ./a.out
test.f90:5:16:

    5 |     REAL F, ONE=1, TWO=2
      |                1
Error: Syntax error in data declaration at (1)
```

## Concern

The concern of this issue *isn't* the **error not reported** bug, but rather the fact that this allows an incorrect way to initialize variables and hence might disturb other correct fixes to the parser. With this issue the main concern is to correct the `tests/fixedform_doloop3.f`, below is a screenshot from https://bayanbox.ir/view/3820206914900746365/StatLab-Applied-Statistics-Algorithms.pdf, where the program `tests/fixedform_doloop3.f` comes from:

![Screenshot 2024-11-11 at 6 14 28 PM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/16ed9290-fb07-4715-9129-cfe595d7881b)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2468989275","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341#issuecomment-2468989275","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5341","id":2468989275,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6TKclb","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-11T20:43:05Z","updated_at":"2024-11-11T20:43:05Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Is this the same bug as #3945 ? ```
(lf) john:~$ gunzip lfortran-0.42.0.tar.gz
(lf) john:~$ tar -x -f lfortran-0.42.0.tar
(lf) john:~$ conda create -n lf
WARNING: A conda environment already exists at '/home/john/miniforge3/envs/lf'

Remove existing environment?
This will remove ALL directories contained within this specified prefix directory, including any other conda environments.

 (y/[n])? n

CondaSystemExit: Exiting.

(lf) john:~$  conda activate lf
(lf) john:~$  conda install lfortran -c conda-forge
Channels:
 - conda-forge
 - https://software.repos.intel.com/python/conda
Platform: linux-64
Collecting package metadata (repodata.json): done
Solving environment: done

# All requested packages already installed.

(lf) john:~$ lfortran --version
LFortran version: 0.41.0
Platform: Linux
Default target: x86_64-conda-linux-gnu
(lf) john:~$ 
```
So I seem to still have the old version. I found that the ```tar -x -f``` command above had generated a directory ```lfortran-0.42.0``` and wondered if it was in a place where ```conda install``` couldn't find it.

So I entered at the command line
```
(lf) john:~$ du | grep 0.41.0
```
to discover where the old version was. That revealed it in two places:
```
./miniforge3/pkgs/lfortran-0.41.0-h4f3f773_1
./conda_root/pkgs/lfortran-0.41.0-h4f3f773_1
```
Therefore I tried this, with the results shown:
```
(lf) john:~$ mv lfortran-0.42.0 conda_root/pkgs
(lf) john:~$ conda install lfortran -c conda-forge
Retrieving notices: ...working... done
Channels:
 - conda-forge
Platform: linux-64
Collecting package metadata (repodata.json): done
Solving environment: done

## Package Plan ##

  added / updated specs:
    - lfortran

    package                    |            build
    ---------------------------|-----------------
    lfortran-0.41.0            |       h4f3f773_2        23.7 MB  conda-forge
    ------------------------------------------------------------
                                           Total:        23.7 MB

The following packages will be UPDATED:

  lfortran                                0.41.0-h4f3f773_1 --> 0.41.0-h4f3f773_2 


Proceed ([y]/n)? y 


Downloading and Extracting Packages:
                                                                                
Preparing transaction: done
Verifying transaction: done
Executing transaction: done
(lf) john:~$ lfortran --version
LFortran version: 0.41.0
Platform: Linux
Default target: x86_64-conda-linux-gnu
(lf) john:~$ 
```
Conclusion: I still hadn't got version 0.42.0. Now tried this, with the results shown:
```
(lf) john:~$ mv ./conda_root/pkgs/lfortran-0.42.0 ./miniforge3/pkgs
(lf) john:~$ conda install lfortran -c conda-forge
Channels:
 - conda-forge
 - https://software.repos.intel.com/python/conda
Platform: linux-64
Collecting package metadata (repodata.json): done

(lf) john:~$ lfortran --version
LFortran version: 0.41.0
Platform: Linux
Default target: x86_64-conda-linux-gnu
(lf) john:~$ 
```
I am a Fortran user and programmer, not a systems programmer. I have no expertise in conda or miniforge3. How do I get the latest version of lfortran?

Minimal Reproducible Example

```fortran
program main
   integer, parameter :: myInt=1e7
end program
```

### LLVM IR
```llvm
; ModuleID = 'LFortran'
source_filename = "LFortran"

define i32 @main(i32 %0, i8** %1) {
.entry:
  %myint = alloca i32, align 4
  store i32 10000000, i32* %myint, align 4
  call void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32 %0, i8** %1)
  %myint1 = alloca i32, align 4
  store i32 10000000, i32* %myint1, align 4
  br label %return

return:                                           ; preds = %.entry
  ret i32 0
}

declare void @_lpython_call_initial_functions(i32, i8**)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5440/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5440/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2494807313","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5440#issuecomment-2494807313","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5440","id":2494807313,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Us70R","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-22T21:07:49Z","updated_at":"2024-11-22T21:07:49Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"My system found no bug in lfortran's treatment of that program! function"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-06-13T20:42:51Z","updated_at":"2024-10-04T22:43:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This program is supposed to print the result of transferring the double precision signaling and quiet NANs to integer arrays. It prints one integer for each instead of the expected two, the values it prints are equal, and if lfortran 0.36.0 is used again quite different values are printed, as  is shown below. The program ```nandp.f90``` is:
```
   use ieee_arithmetic
  implicit none
  double precision dpnan(2)
  dpnan = ieee_value(dpnan, [ieee_signaling_nan,ieee_quiet_nan] )
  print "(A,2(1X,I0))",'IEEE signaling NaN =', transfer(dpnan(1),[1])
  print "(A,2(1X,I0))",'IEEE quiet     NaN =', transfer(dpnan(2),[1])
end program
```
The output from compiling and running it 3 times:
```
lf) john:~/Test$ lfortran nandp.f90
IEEE signaling NaN = 4201219760 
IEEE quiet     NaN = 4201219760 
(lf) john:~/Test$ lfortran nandp.f90
IEEE signaling NaN = 2383303968 
IEEE quiet     NaN = 2383303968 
(lf) john:~/Test$ lfortran nandp.f90
IEEE signaling NaN = 1308315952 
IEEE quiet     NaN = 1308315952 
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4258/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4258/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2394758905","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4258#issuecomment-2394758905","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4258","id":2394758905,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6OvR75","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-04T22:43:33Z","updated_at":"2024-10-04T22:43:33Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"This bug has been attacked but not killed. {"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501","id":2406728379,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6Pc8K7","number":4501,"title":"segmentation fault for array section of non-default integer kind","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-07-13T06:13:24Z","updated_at":"2024-10-17T20:15:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The only difference between these two programs is in their lines defining the integer k, which is used as the kind of 1_k. The first program segfaults and the second runs happily. Why?
Evidence from lfortran 0.36.0 in an x86_64 Ubuntu 22.04 system when k is 1:
```
(lf) john:~/Test$ cat arraysectionc.f90
  implicit none
  integer,parameter:: k = selected_int_kind(0) ! smallest integer type
  integer :: iarray(3,4)
  iarray(1,1:2) = [bit_size(1_k),huge(1_k)]
  print *,kind(1_k),iarray(1,1:2)
end program
(lf) john:~/Test$ lfortran arraysectionc.f90
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
(lf) john:~/Test$ 
```
But when k is 4:
```
(lf) john:~/Test$ cat arraysectiond.f90
  implicit none
  integer,parameter:: k = selected_int_kind(9) ! default integer
  integer :: iarray(3,4)
  iarray(1,1:2) = [bit_size(1_k),huge(1_k)]
  print *,kind(1_k),iarray(1,1:2)
end program
(lf) john:~/Test$ lfortran arraysectiond.f90
4 32 2147483647 
(lf) john:~/Test$","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501/reactions","total_count":2,"+1":2,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2420470729","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501#issuecomment-2420470729","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4501","id":2420470729,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6QRXPJ","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-17T20:15:17Z","updated_at":"2024-10-17T20:15:17Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Thank you Harshita. I think that assignment is valid Fortran, and so 
lfortran should cope with it, because huge(1_k) < huge(1). Certainly
scalar of kind 4 = scalar of kind 1 causes no problem for lfortran.

On Thu, 17 Oct 2024, Harshita Kalani wrote:

> Date: Thu, 17 Oct 2024 12:38:52 +0000
> From: Harshita Kalani ***@***.***>
> Reply-To: lfortran/lfortran
>     ***@***.***>
> To: lfortran/lfortran ***@***.***>
> Cc: harperjf ***@***.***>, Author ***@***.***>
> Subject: Re: [lfortran/lfortran] segmentation fault for array section of
>     non-default integer kind (Issue #4501)
> 
> This is because, in the first program, iarray is defined as integer of default kind which is 4.
> So the first program fails because of:
> ```
> iarray(1,1:2) = [bit_size(1_k),huge(1_k)]
> ```
> `array of kind 4 = array of kind 1`
>

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5143","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5143/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5143/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5143/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5143","id":2609982523,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6bkSw7","number":5143,"title":"Generalizing iachar in 2 different ways gives seg.faults","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-23T22:44:47Z","updated_at":"2024-10-23T22:44:47Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Having seen some discussion of ```iachar('AB')``` recently, I wondered if one could overload ```iachar``` to allow strings longer than 1 character. The answer is no because length of a character variable is not a kind type parameter.
So I wrote two little functions ```iascii(string)``` to write the ASCII codes of string into a one-dimensional integer array, and  ```ascii(codes)``` to write a string using the ASCII codes of an integer scalar or array. This is version A = module + test program:
```
module asciimod ! f95-compliant file asciiA.f90: [] only in character strings 
  implicit none
  public iascii,ascii
  interface ascii
     module procedure asciiarray,asciiscalar
  end interface ascii
contains 
  pure function  iascii(   string)
    character,intent(in):: string*(*)
    integer      iascii(len(string))
    iascii = iachar(transfer(string,(/'A'/))) 
  end function iascii

  pure function asciiarray(codes)
    integer,intent(in)::   codes(:)
    character   asciiarray*(size(codes))
    asciiarray = transfer(achar(codes),asciiarray)
  end function asciiarray
  
  pure function asciiscalar(codes) 
    integer,intent(in)::    codes
    character   asciiscalar*1
    asciiscalar = asciiarray((/codes/))
  end function asciiscalar
  
end module asciimod

program asciiA
  use asciimod, only: iascii,ascii
  implicit none
  integer empty(0)
  write(*,*) "iascii('a') = [",iascii('a'),']'
  write(*,*) "ascii( 97 ) = '"//ascii( 97 )//"'"
  write(*,*) "ascii([97]) = '"//ascii((/97/))//"'"
  write(*,*) "ascii(iascii('a')) = '"//ascii(iascii('a'))//"'"
  write(*,*) "iascii('abc') = [",iascii('abc'),']'
  write(*,*) "ascii(iascii('abc')) = '"//ascii(iascii('abc'))//"'"
  write(*,*) "iascii('') = [",iascii(''),']'
  write(*,*) "ascii(iascii('')) = '"//ascii(iascii(''))//"'"
  write(*,*) "ascii([97,98,99]) = '"//ascii((/97,98,99/))//"'"
  write(*,*) "ascii(empty) = '"//ascii(empty)//"'"
  write(*,*) "iascii(ascii(empty)) = [",iascii(ascii(empty)),']'
  write(*,*) "iascii(ascii([1,2,3])) = [",iascii(ascii((/1,2,3/))),']'
end program asciiA
```
It compiled and ran with gfortran, g95, ifx,ifort and AMD flang but lfortran 0.41.0 gave a compile-time seg.fault. Suspecting that my previous trouble with ```transfer``` (#3961) may have been the problem I tried the same test program with version B of the module, which has do loops instead of transfers:
```
module asciimod ! f95-compliant file asciiB.f90: [] only in character strings
  implicit none
  public iascii,ascii
  interface ascii
     module procedure asciiarray,asciiscalar
  end interface ascii
contains 
  pure function iascii(    string)
    character,intent(in):: string*(*)
    integer     iascii(len(string)), i
    do i = 1, len(string)
       iascii(i) = iachar(string(i:i))
    end do
  end function iascii

  pure function asciiarray(      codes)
    integer,intent(in)::         codes(:)
    character   asciiarray*(size(codes))
    integer i
    do i = 1, size(codes)
       asciiarray(i:i) = achar(codes(i))
    end do
  end function asciiarray

  pure function asciiscalar(codes) 
    integer,intent(in)::    codes
    character   asciiscalar*1
    asciiscalar = asciiarray((/codes/))
  end function asciiscalar

end module asciimod
```
That gave the same results as before. Lfortran's error message did not say which Fortran statement(s) caused the seg.fault. Is it #3960 again?

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4726","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4726/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4726/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4726/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4726","id":2499310892,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6U-HUs","number":4726,"title":"Array sections can evaluate a variable, but not a constant ","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-01T08:36:20Z","updated_at":"2024-09-01T08:36:20Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"These programs show that lfortran supports array sections used to evaluate a variable ```nkr``` but has an ICE  if ```nkr``` is a constant. They are part of my project to write one program that finds the Fortran-defined properties of numbers of various kinds that compiles and runs with all my compilers.

Program with ```nkr``` variable:
```
program kindtest
  implicit none
  integer,parameter::  kr1 = selected_real_kind(1), maxkr = 3, &
       skr2 = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_kr1)+1), &
       kr2  = merge(skr2,kr1, skr2>0),                & 
       skr3 = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_kr2)+1), &
       kr3  = merge(skr3,kr2, skr3>0),&
! kr3 = skr3 if that's a valid real kind, kr2 if not
       allkr(maxkr+1) = [kr1,kr2,kr3,kr3]
  integer:: nkr
  nkr = minloc(abs(allkr(1:maxkr)-allkr(2:maxkr+1)),1)
  
  call realkinds(nkr)
contains
  subroutine realkinds( nkr)
    integer,intent(in)::nkr
    integer :: n
    integer:: goodkr(nkr) !!= [(allkr(n),n=1,nkr)]
    goodkr = allkr(1:nkr)
    print *,'precision(1.0_kr1) =',precision(1.0_kr1)
    if(nkr>=2) print *,'precision(1.0_kr2) =',precision(1.0_kr2)
    if(nkr>=3) print *,'precision(1.0_kr3) =',precision(1.0_kr3)
    write(*,"(A,1X,I0,A)",advance='no') &
	'Number of different real kinds =',nkr,', real kinds:'
    do n = 1,nkr ! lfortran can't use implied-do or array section
       write(*,"(1X,I0)",advance=merge('no ','yes',n<nkr)) allkr(n)
    end do
    print "(3A)",'There ',merge('may be','are no',nkr>=maxkr),' more real kinds'
  end subroutine realkinds
end program kindtest
```
Run-time output from lfortran 0.40.0:
```
precision(1.0_kr1) = 6
precision(1.0_kr2) = 15
Number of different real kinds = 2, real kinds: 4 8
There are no more real kinds
```
Program with ```nkr``` constant:
```
program kindtest
  implicit none
  integer,parameter::  kr1 = selected_real_kind(1), maxkr = 3, &       skr2 = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_kr1)+1), &
       kr2  = merge(skr2,kr1, skr2>0),                & 
       skr3 = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_kr2)+1), &
       kr3  = merge(skr3,kr2, skr3>0),&
! kr3 = skr3 if that's a valid real kind, kr2 if not
       allkr(maxkr+1) = [kr1,kr2,kr3,kr3],&
       nkr = minloc(abs(allkr(1:maxkr)-allkr(2:maxkr+1)),1)
  
  call realkinds(nkr)
contains
  subroutine realkinds( nkr)
    integer,intent(in)::nkr
    integer :: n
    integer:: goodkr(nkr) !!= [(allkr(n),n=1,nkr)]
    goodkr = allkr(1:nkr)
    print "(A,I0)",'precision(1.0_kr1) = ',precision(1.0_kr1)
    if(nkr>=2) print "(A,I0)",'precision(1.0_kr2) = ',precision(1.0_kr2)
    if(nkr>=3) print "(A,I0)",'precision(1.0_kr3) = ',precision(1.0_kr3)
    write(*,"(A,I0,A)",advance='no') &
         'Number of different real kinds = ',nkr,', real kinds:'
    do n = 1,nkr ! lfortran write can't use implied-do or array section
       write(*,"(1X,I0)",advance=merge('no ','yes',n<nkr)) allkr(n)
    end do
    print "(3A)",'There ',merge('may be','are no',nkr>=maxkr),' more real kinds'
  end subroutine realkinds
end program kindtest
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4730","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4730/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4730/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4730/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4730","id":2500057098,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6VA9gK","number":4730,"title":"ES, EN, E formats with repeat counts fail","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-02T05:49:55Z","updated_at":"2024-09-02T05:49:55Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This program is valid Fortran 95 but lfortran 0.40.0 can't compile it.
```
 print "(2ES13.3)", epsilon(1e0),epsilon(1d0)
end program
```
Compile-time output:
```
Unsupported or unrecognized `S` in format string
```
If ES is changed to EN, the compile-time output becomes
```
Unsupported or unrecognized `N` in format string
```
If ES is changed to E, the program runs happily. But if ```2E13.3``` is changed to
```2E13.3E2``` there is a different wrong compile-time error message:
```
Error: Period required in format specifier
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4730/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4730/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

The Fortran 2023 standard says that the result of these functions shall be a scalar (see 17.11.52 for example) even if their argument is an array. I now think that lfortran needs amendment because an elemental function must return an array in that situation. This program shows why:
```
  use ieee_arithmetic, only: ieee_support_datatype
  real x(10)
  print *,ieee_support_datatype(x)
```
Output from lfortran 0.40.0:
```
 T    T    T    T    T    T    T    T    T    T
```
Output from gfortran, g95, ifort, ifx and AMD flang:
```
 T
```
except that flang put an extra space before the ```T```.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2325389582/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4742","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4742/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4742/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4742/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4742","id":2501793558,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6VHlcW","number":4742,"title":"ISO_FORTRAN_ENV items that lfortran should have:","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-03T02:12:46Z","updated_at":"2024-09-03T02:12:46Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"From F2003:
character_storage_size, file_storage_size, iostat_eor, numeric_storage_size

From F2008:
iostat_inquire_internal_unit, stat_locked, stat_locked_other_image, stat_unlocked, stat_stopped_image, compiler_version, compiler_options

From 2018:
atomic_int_kind, atomic_logical_kind, current_team, initial_team, parent_team, stat_failed_image, stat_unlocked_failed_image

From F2023:
logical8, logical16, logical32, logical64, real16

The items I often use with other compilers:
character_storage_size, file_storage_size, numeric_storage_size, compiler_version, compiler_options

Lfortran iso_fortran_env has integer_kinds (from F2018) but it's wrong because the correct answer is [1, 2, 4, 8].

There are things in the standard but not in gfortran. In particular https://gcc.gnu.org/onlinedocs/gfortran/GET_005fCOMMAND.html told me that get_command has 3 arguments, all optional (correct in F2008 but not now). I think lfortran should follow the standard, not repeat the obsolete parts of another compiler, however good it is.   Another example: ifx has implemented all the F2018 items of iso_fortran_env but gfortran omitted some. I use both because each does some things better than the other, and I have sent many bug reports when compilers do different things with the same program.

(Some IEEE functions may have an argument of any rank but a scalar function value.)  Unfortunately lfortran does not yet provide it. This program shows the problem and its solution:
```
 implicit none
  real x(2,2,2)
  print *,'assumed rank:',assumed(x)
  print *,'elemental   :',elementl(x)
contains
  pure logical function assumed(x)
    real,intent(in):: x(..)
    assumed = .true.
  end function assumed
  
  elemental logical function elementl(x)
    real,intent(in):: x
    elementl = .true.
  end function elementl
end program
```
Run-time output from gfortran, ifx or ifort:
```
 assumed rank: T
 elemental   : T T T T T T T T
```

The old version gave a seg.fault but this one gives an ICE whether or not --std=f23 is used.
```
! allocatesource.f90 ! allocate with source=- is a F2008 feature
program allocatesource
  character(*),parameter:: fmt = "(A,2(1X,I0))"
  call test( n= 100 )
  print fmt,'expected   result',100,100
  call test( n= 200 )
  print fmt,'expected   result',200,200
  print "(A)",'But gfortran or ifx output was','subroutine result 100 100',&
       'expected   result 100 100','subroutine result 200 200', &
       'expected   result 200 200'
contains
  subroutine test( n )
    integer, intent(in) :: n
    integer, allocatable :: arr(:)
    allocate( arr( 2 ), source= n )
    print fmt,'subroutine result',arr
    !! explicit deallocation not needed unless arr saved
  end subroutine test
end program allocatesource
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4768/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4768/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

The ```do``` loop and ```advance=``` options in the ```write``` statements were to avoid a different lfortran bug.
```
program kindtest
  implicit none
  integer,parameter::  kr1 = selected_real_kind(1), maxkr = 3, &
       skr2 = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_kr1)+1), &
       kr2  = merge(skr2,kr1, skr2>0),                & 
       skr3 = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_kr2)+1), &
       kr3  = merge(skr3,kr2, skr3>0),&
! kr3 = skr3 if that's a valid real kind, kr2 if not
       allkr(maxkr+1) = [kr1,kr2,kr3,kr3],&
       nkr = minloc(abs(allkr(1:maxkr)-allkr(2:maxkr+1)),1)
  
  call realkinds(nkr)
contains
  subroutine realkinds( nkr)
    integer,intent(in)::nkr
    integer :: n
    integer:: goodkr(nkr) !!= [(allkr(n),n=1,nkr)]
    goodkr = allkr(1:nkr)
    print "(A,I0)",'precision(1.0_kr1) = ',precision(1.0_kr1)
    if(nkr>=2) print "(A,I0)",'precision(1.0_kr2) = ',precision(1.0_kr2)
    if(nkr>=3) print "(A,I0)",'precision(1.0_kr3) = ',precision(1.0_kr3)
    write(*,"(A,I0,A)",advance='no') &
         'Number of different real kinds = ',nkr,', real kinds:'
    do n = 1,nkr ! lfortran write can't use implied-do or array section
       write(*,"(1X,I0)",advance=merge('no ','yes',n<nkr)) allkr(n)
    end do
    print "(3A)",'There ',merge('may be','are no',nkr>=maxkr),' more real kinds'
  end subroutine realkinds
end program kindtest
```

program writeteste
  character:: c(6)*3
  data c/'ab ','cd ','ef ','gh ','ij ','kl '/
  write(*,"(*(A))") 'c = "',c,'"'

  write(*,"(*(A))") 'c = "',c,'"'
end program writeteste
```

```
c = "ab ab ab ab ab ab "

c = "ab cd ef gh ij kl "

```
c = "ab cd ef gh ij kl "

c = "ab cd ef gh ij kl "

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4925","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4925/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4925/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4925/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4925","id":2552388940,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6YIl1M","number":4925,"title":"ES11.2E4 treated as ES11.2E2","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-27T08:56:14Z","updated_at":"2024-09-27T08:56:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This program is part of an effort to modify my program that finds properties of real and integer kinds so that lfortran will
accept it. It already works well with 4 other compilers and finds bugs in 2 more, including lfortran which gives a wrong answer for ```tiny``` and a seg.fault with ```huge```, partly because it treats ES11.2E4 format as if it were ES11.2E2. It looks like the opposite bug to the one reported in #4530.
```
program estest
  implicit none
  integer,parameter:: sp = kind(1e0), dp = kind(1d0),&
       sep = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_dp)+1),&
       ep = merge(sep,dp,sep>0) ! extended precision if compiler allows
  real(sp),parameter:: xsp = 1
  real(dp),parameter:: xdp = 1 
  real(ep),parameter:: xep = 1
  write(*,"(A)")            'kind             sp         dp         ep'
  write(*,"(A,3(ES11.2E4))")'epsilon:',epsilon(xsp),epsilon(xdp),epsilon(xep)
  write(*,"(A,3(ES11.2E4))")'tiny   :',   tiny(xsp),   tiny(xdp),   tiny(xep)
  write(*,"(A,3(ES11.2E4))")'huge   :',   huge(xsp),   huge(xdp),   huge(xep)
end program estest
```
Output from lfortran 0.40.0, in which dp == ep:
```
kind             sp         dp         ep
epsilon:   1.19E-07   2.22E-16   2.22E-16
tiny   :   1.18E-38   0.00E-01   0.00E-01
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```
Output from gfortran or g95, in which dp /= ep:
```
kind             sp         dp         ep
epsilon: 1.19E-0007 2.22E-0016 1.08E-0019
tiny   : 1.18E-0038 2.23E-0308 3.36E-4932
huge   : 3.40E+0038 1.80E+0308 1.19E+4932
```
Output from ifx or ifort, in which ep is different from gfortran's:
```
kind             sp         dp         ep
epsilon: 1.19E-0007 2.22E-0016 1.93E-0034
tiny   : 1.18E-0038 2.23E-0308 3.36E-4932
huge   : 3.40E+0038 1.80E+0308 1.19E+4932
```
Output from AMD flang, revealing bugs both like and different from lfortran's:
```
kind             sp         dp         ep
epsilon:   1.19E-07   2.22E-16   1.93E-34
tiny   :   1.18E-38   2.23-308   0.00E+00
huge   :   3.40E+38   1.80+308        Inf
```

working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-09-27T08:56:14Z","updated_at":"2024-09-27T21:52:44Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This program is part of an effort to modify my program that finds properties of real and integer kinds so that lfortran will
accept it. It already works well with 4 other compilers and finds bugs in 2 more, including lfortran which gives a wrong answer for ```tiny``` and a seg.fault with ```huge```, partly because it treats ES11.2E4 format as if it were ES11.2E2. It looks like the opposite bug to the one reported in #4530.
```
program estest
  implicit none
  integer,parameter:: sp = kind(1e0), dp = kind(1d0),&
       sep = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_dp)+1),&
       ep = merge(sep,dp,sep>0) ! extended precision if compiler allows
  real(sp),parameter:: xsp = 1
  real(dp),parameter:: xdp = 1 
  real(ep),parameter:: xep = 1
  write(*,"(A)")            'kind             sp         dp         ep'
  write(*,"(A,3(ES11.2E4))")'epsilon:',epsilon(xsp),epsilon(xdp),epsilon(xep)
  write(*,"(A,3(ES11.2E4))")'tiny   :',   tiny(xsp),   tiny(xdp),   tiny(xep)
  write(*,"(A,3(ES11.2E4))")'huge   :',   huge(xsp),   huge(xdp),   huge(xep)
end program estest
```
Output from lfortran 0.40.0, in which dp == ep:
```
kind             sp         dp         ep
epsilon:   1.19E-07   2.22E-16   2.22E-16
tiny   :   1.18E-38   0.00E-01   0.00E-01
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```
Output from gfortran or g95, in which dp /= ep:
```
kind             sp         dp         ep
epsilon: 1.19E-0007 2.22E-0016 1.08E-0019
tiny   : 1.18E-0038 2.23E-0308 3.36E-4932
huge   : 3.40E+0038 1.80E+0308 1.19E+4932
```
Output from ifx or ifort, in which ep is different from gfortran's:
```
kind             sp         dp         ep
epsilon: 1.19E-0007 2.22E-0016 1.93E-0034
tiny   : 1.18E-0038 2.23E-0308 3.36E-4932
huge   : 3.40E+0038 1.80E+0308 1.19E+4932
```
Output from AMD flang, revealing bugs both like and different from lfortran's:
```
kind             sp         dp         ep
epsilon:   1.19E-07   2.22E-16   1.93E-34
tiny   :   1.18E-38   2.23-308   0.00E+00
huge   :   3.40E+38   1.80+308        Inf
```

It worked like a charm with gfortran, g95 (now over 10 years old), ifx, ifort and AMD flang. But the error message from lfortran does not tell the user what it objected to. The program:
```
program es_etest !f95 program; compile-time segfault with lfortran, OK g95 etc
  implicit none  !(merge disallowed in f95 initialization expressions) 
  integer,parameter:: sp = kind(1e0), dp = kind(1d0),&
       sep = selected_real_kind(precision(1.0_dp)+1),&
       ep = (sep+dp+sign(1,sep)*(sep-dp))/2 ! extended real if compiler has it 
  character(11) values(3)
  real(sp),parameter:: xsp = 1
  real(dp),parameter:: xdp = 1 
  real(ep),parameter:: xep = 1
  write(*,"(A9,3A12)") 'kind name','sp','dp','ep'
  write(*,"(A9,3I12)") 'kind:',sp, dp, ep
  values = (/ es(real(epsilon(xsp),ep),11,2,4), & ! avoid recursive I/O
       es(real(epsilon(xdp),ep),11,2,4),es(real(epsilon(xep),ep),11,2,4) /)
  write(*,"(4(1X,A))") 'epsilon:',values
  values = (/ es(real(   tiny(xsp),ep),11,2,4), &
       es(real(   tiny(xdp),ep),11,2,4),es(real(   tiny(xep),ep),11,2,4) /)
  write(*,"(4(1X,A))") '   tiny:',values
  values = (/ es(real(   huge(xsp),ep),11,2,4), &
       es(real(   huge(xdp),ep),11,2,4),es(real(   huge(xep),ep),11,2,4) /)
  write(*,"(4(1X,A))") '   huge:',values
contains
  function cn(n) ! n -> character string 
    integer,intent(in)::n
    character(range(n)+2) cn
    write(cn,"(I0)") n
    cn = adjustl(cn)
  end function cn
    
  function es(x,w,d,e) ! write real(ep) x in format ESw.dEe; f95-compliant
    real(ep),intent(in):: x
    integer,intent(in) :: w,d,e
    character(w) es, ce, c_aftere, ctenpower, csigfigs
    character(1) cfirstdigit, cesign, csignx, claterdigits*(d+1)
    real(ep) signx, absx, scaledx
    integer pointplace,eplace,digitsaftere,tenpower,k ! scale by k digits
    signx = sign(1.0_ep,x)
    csignx = merge(' ','-',signx>0.0_ep)

    if(x==0.0_ep)then
       es = adjustr(csignx//'0.'//repeat('0',d)//'E+'//repeat('0',e))
       return
    end if

    absx = abs(x) ! Reached here if x /= 0
    k = int(log10(absx))
    scaledx = x/10.0_ep**k
    write(ce,"(E"//trim(cn(w))//"."//trim(cn(d+1))//")") scaledx
    eplace = index(ce,'E')
    pointplace = index(ce,'.')
    csigfigs = adjustl(ce(pointplace+1:eplace-1))
    c_aftere = adjustl(trim(ce(eplace+1:)))
    read(c_aftere,*) digitsaftere
    tenpower = digitsaftere + k - 1
    cesign = merge('+','-',tenpower>=0)
    ctenpower = adjustl(trim(cn(abs(tenpower))))
    cfirstdigit = csigfigs(1:1)
    claterdigits = csigfigs(2:)
    es = csignx//cfirstdigit//'.'//trim(claterdigits)//'E'//cesign// &
         repeat('0',e-len_trim(ctenpower))//trim(ctenpower)
  end function es
end program es_etest
```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906","id":2252450328,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6GQaoY","number":3906,"title":"x = huge(x) disallowed in a constant expression","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-04-19T08:53:44Z","updated_at":"2024-08-06T21:57:52Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The declaration of x in this program appears to be standard-conforming, by F2018 10.1.12 para 2. Five compilers including g95 allowed it, but lfortran did not:
```
(lf2) john:~/Test/Glist$ cat bigdeclare.f90
program bigdeclare
  implicit none
  real(kind(1d0)):: x = huge(x)
  print *,x
end program bigdeclare
(lf2) john:~/Test/Glist$ lfortran bigdeclare.f90
semantic error: Variable 'x' is not declared
 --> bigdeclare.f90:3:30
  |
3 |   real(kind(1d0)):: x = huge(x)
  |                              ^ 'x' is undeclared


Note: Please report unclear or confusing messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
(lf2) john:~/Test/Glist$ 
```
That paragraph in the F2018 standard says
"If a constant expression includes a specification inquiry that depends on a type parameter or an array bound of an entity specified in the same specification-part, the type parameter or array bound shall be specified in a prior specification of the specification-part. The prior specification may be to the left of the specification inquiry in the same statement, but shall not be within the same entity-decl unless the specification inquiry appears within an initialization."

Here the prior specification is the first x, and huge(x) is a specification inquiry.
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2272221029","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906#issuecomment-2272221029","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3906","id":2272221029,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6Hb1dl","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-08-06T21:57:51Z","updated_at":"2024-08-06T21:57:51Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Lfortran said this program had a semantic error but is it really an error in the intrinsic ```huge``` ? I get the same error if ```huge``` is replaced by ```epsilon``` or ```tiny```; all three intrinsics require the kind but not the value of ```x``` to be known beforehand. Judicious commenting out will show that it also fails for kind ```int16``` but it runs correctly with ```int32``` and ```int64```. The program:
```
program ndigits
  use iso_fortran_env, only: int8,int16,int32,int64 
  implicit none
  integer:: n
  print *,digits(1_int8 )
  print *,digits(1_int16)
  print *,digits(1_int32)
  print *,digits(1_int64)
end program ndigits
```
Output from gfortran:
```
           7
          15
          31
          63
```
Output from lfortran:
```
semantic error: Kind 1 not supported for type Integer
 --> ndigits.f90:5:11
  |
5 |   print *,digits(1_int8 )
  |           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ 


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4622/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4622/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4637","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4637/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4637/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4637/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4637","id":2460219753,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6So_lp","number":4637,"title":"Two bugs: READ from terminal","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-12T07:16:25Z","updated_at":"2024-08-12T07:16:25Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This program is supposed to read an integer from the terminal and check it, twice.. If the first input is not an integer, lfortran neither gives an ```iomsg``` (bug 1) nor waits for the user to enter the next integer (bug 2). The ```i = 1,2``` in the ```do```  statement prevented an infinite loop at run time.
Gfortran does the right thing: if the first input is not an integer, the ```iomsg``` is correct, and the second input is correctly treated. The program below resembles the one in #4357 but that one did not mention ```iomsg```. See also #4438.
```
program readtesta
  implicit none ! lfortran problem with iostat in read statement
  integer  :: i,n,ios
  character:: msg*200
  character(:),allocatable:: iomsg
do i = 1,2
  print *,'Enter an integer'
  read(*,* ,iostat=ios,iomsg=msg) n
  print *,'n=',n
  iomsg = '"'//trim(msg)//'"'
  if(ios>0) print *,'Error reading integer: iostat=',ios,'iomsg=',iomsg
  if(ios<0) print *,'Insufficient input: iostat=',ios,'iomsg=',iomsg
end do
end program readtesta
```
Output from lfortran 0.38.0:
```
Enter an integer
BAD
n= 832483328
Enter an integer
n= 832483328
```
Output from gfortran:
```
 Enter an integer
BAD
 n=           0
 Error reading integer: iostat=        5010 iomsg="Bad integer for item 1 in list input"
 Enter an integer
666
 n=         666
```

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4640","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4640/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4640/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4640/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4640","id":2462079750,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6SwFsG","number":4640,"title":"inquire by I/O list not implemented?","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-12T23:49:25Z","updated_at":"2024-08-12T23:49:25Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This program tests the "inquire by I/O list" statement for a variable of these 6 kinds: int8, int16, int32, int64, real32, real64. Lfortran does not set the ```iolength``` value in any case but 4 other compilers deal correctly with all 6 of them. The program:
```
program iolen
  use iso_fortran_env, only: int8, int16, int32, int64, real32, real64
  implicit none
  integer:: i,len(6) = -huge(1)
  inquire(iolength=len(1)) 42_int8
  inquire(iolength=len(2)) 42_int16
  inquire(iolength=len(3)) 42_int32
  inquire(iolength=len(4)) 42_int64
  inquire(iolength=len(5)) 4.2_real32
  inquire(iolength=len(6)) 4.2_real64
  print "(6(I0,1X))", len
end program iolen
```
Lfortran 0.38.0 output:
```
-2147483647 -2147483647 -2147483647 -2147483647 -2147483647 -2147483647
```
AMD flang, gfortran, ifort or ifx output:
```
1 2 4 8 4 8
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4640/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4640/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4646","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4646/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4646/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4646/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/4646","id":2464927137,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6S682h","number":4646,"title":"Option needed to get standard compliance; unclear documentation","user":{"login":"harperjf","id":25044934,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI1MDQ0OTM0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/25044934?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf","html_url":"https://github.com/harperjf","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/harperjf/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-08-14T05:23:18Z","updated_at":"2024-08-14T05:23:18Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This program gives standard-conforming output with lfortran if and only if the option ```--print-leading-space``` is used.
```
program space1
  write(*,*) 'Hello world!'
  write(*,"(A)") 'Hello again!'
end program space1
```
I am one of the users who prefers standard-conforming behaviour without compile-time options, rather than needing them to get it in some circumstances. #2672 recommended the Python system (does it omit the leading space?)  but Fortran programs should follow the Fortran standard. With format ```*``` it requires the leading space and gives the compiler a lot of choice after that.
 
The lfortran documentation says
```
--print-leading-space: Print leading white space if format is unspecified
```
That is unclear because (a) PRINT and WRITE are different statements and (b) there are three ways to use a WRITE statement:
unformatted, or with format ```*```, or with an explicit format e.g. "(A)".
PRINT does not allow the unformatted case. I had thought "format is unspecified" meant the unformatted case for a WRITE statement because the other two cases both specify a format. I therefore suggest that the documentation be amended to
```
--print-leading-space: Print leading white space if the format is *
```
But my preference would be to make ```--print-leading-space``` the default and have a new option
```
--no-leading-space: Omit the leading space when writing with format *
```
